title: Europe Faces Deadly Heatwave Crisis as Temperatures Move East
subtitle: As the extreme heat shifts, countries in central and eastern Europe prepare for record-breaking conditions.

summary.en: A severe heatwave is currently moving across central and eastern Europe, bringing dangerous temperatures to many countries. Authorities have issued red alerts as cities like Budapest expect to reach 40 degrees Celsius. Meanwhile, France is dealing with the tragic aftermath of the heat, which has caused over 1,000 excess deaths. Funeral directors in Paris are struggling to find space for the dead as mortuaries reach capacity. Scientists warn that this extreme weather is a direct result of the climate crisis.
summary.zh: 一股嚴重的熱浪目前正橫掃中歐與東歐，為許多國家帶來危險的高溫。當局已發布紅色警報，預計布達佩斯等城市的氣溫將達到攝氏四十度。同時，法國正處理熱浪帶來的悲慘後果，該事件已造成超過一千人超額死亡。巴黎的殯葬業者因停屍間達到飽和，正苦於尋找安置遺體的空間。科學家警告，這種極端天氣是氣候危機的直接結果。

paragraphs.en: A dangerous heatwave is moving across central and eastern Europe, bringing record-breaking temperatures to a new part of the continent. After causing significant loss of life in western Europe, the "heat dome"—a high-pressure system that traps hot air like a lid on a pot—has shifted east. According to The Guardian, cities including Budapest, Belgrade, and Bucharest are seeing temperatures reach levels that are both rare and dangerous.
paragraphs.zh: 一股危險的熱浪正橫掃中歐與東歐，為該大陸的新區域帶來破紀錄的高溫。在西歐造成嚴重的人命損失後，「熱穹」（一種將熱空氣像鍋蓋一樣困住的高壓系統）已向東移動。根據《衛報》（The Guardian）報導，包括布達佩斯、貝爾格勒與布加勒斯特在內的城市，氣溫正達到罕見且危險的程度。

paragraphs.en: In Hungary, authorities have issued red warnings for extreme heat. The country’s prime minister, Péter Magyar, used social media to call for national unity, asking citizens to look out for one another during these difficult days. To help those without air conditioning at home, the government has opened more than 2,000 cooling centers across the country. Additionally, the Paks nuclear power plant received special permission to manage its cooling water rules to ensure that electricity production does not drop during this critical time.
paragraphs.zh: 在匈牙利，當局已針對極端高溫發布紅色警報。該國總理馬札爾（Péter Magyar）透過社群媒體呼籲全國團結，要求公民在這段艱難的日子裡互相照應。為了協助家中沒有冷氣的民眾，政府已在全國開設超過兩千個避暑中心。此外，帕克什（Paks）核電廠獲得特別許可，得以調整其冷卻水規範，以確保電力生產在這段關鍵時期不會下降。

paragraphs.en: Other nations are also feeling the pressure. Slovakia recently set a new national temperature record of 40.5C, breaking a mark that had stood since 2007. In Germany, transport systems have faced disruptions as extreme heat caused metal tram tracks to bend. Meanwhile, in Croatia, firefighters are working hard to stop a wildfire on the island of Vis, using aircraft to help protect the area from the flames.
paragraphs.zh: 其他國家也感受到壓力。斯洛伐克最近創下攝氏四十點五度的新全國高溫紀錄，打破自二〇〇七年以來保持的紀錄。在德國，交通系統面臨中斷，因為極端高溫導致金屬電車軌道彎曲。同時，在克羅埃西亞，消防人員正努力撲滅維斯島（Vis）上的野火，並出動飛機協助保護該地區免受火勢威脅。

paragraphs.en: While the heat is currently moving east, western Europe is beginning to count the human cost of the event. In France, public health officials reported that the heatwave contributed to more than 1,000 excess deaths—a term used to describe deaths that are higher than the usual number expected for that time of year. Spain has also recorded more than 800 additional deaths nationwide. The situation in Paris has become particularly difficult for funeral services. According to MedPage Today, mortuaries in the city have run out of space, leaving families in distress as they struggle to find places to store their loved ones. One mortuary owner, Zouhaier Hertelli, described the situation as "catastrophic," noting that he has had to turn away many families because his cold rooms are completely full.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管熱浪目前正向東移動，西歐已開始計算這場事件的人力成本。在法國，公共衛生官員報告稱，熱浪導致超過一千人超額死亡，這是一個用來描述死亡人數高於該季節預期水準的術語。西班牙全國也記錄到超過八百人的額外死亡。巴黎的情況對殯葬服務而言變得特別困難。根據《醫學新聞》（MedPage Today）報導，該市的停屍間已空間不足，導致家屬在尋找安置親人遺體的地方時陷入痛苦。一位停屍間負責人赫泰利（Zouhaier Hertelli）形容情況「災難性」，並指出他不得不拒絕許多家庭，因為他的冷藏室已經完全客滿。

paragraphs.en: Public Health France noted that the death rate in the country spiked significantly during the hottest days of the week. Data shows that 85% of those who died were aged 65 or older, with a 40% increase in deaths occurring at home. Because many of these records are still kept on paper rather than electronically, officials believe the final number of deaths will likely be higher than current estimates. To manage the overflow, Paris city officials have installed temporary storage units and are using hospital space to help funeral directors.
paragraphs.zh: 法國公共衛生署（Public Health France）指出，該國的死亡率在這一週最熱的幾天裡顯著飆升。數據顯示，死者中有百分之八十五為六十五歲或以上，且在家中死亡的人數增加了百分之四十。由於許多紀錄仍以紙本而非電子方式保存，官員認為最終的死亡人數可能會高於目前的估計。為了處理過剩的遺體，巴黎市政府官員已安裝臨時儲存設施，並利用醫院空間協助殯葬業者。

paragraphs.en: Beyond the heat, the extreme weather has also brought violent storms to other parts of the continent. In Italy’s Alto Adige region, heavy rain led to flash floods and landslides. Firefighters were forced to rescue people from their homes and garages after rivers rose quickly and burst their banks. These events serve as a reminder that the current weather patterns are causing a wide range of problems, from fires and floods to record-breaking heat.
paragraphs.zh: 除了高溫之外，極端天氣也為該大陸的其他地區帶來了猛烈的風暴。在義大利的南提洛（Alto Adige）地區，大雨導致了山洪爆發與土石流。由於河水迅速上漲並沖毀堤岸，消防人員被迫將民眾從家中與車庫救出。這些事件提醒人們，當前的天氣型態正引發廣泛的問題，從火災、洪水到破紀錄的高溫皆包含在內。

paragraphs.en: Scientists have stated that this heatwave, which is the most severe and widespread in history, is only possible because of the climate crisis driven by the burning of fossil fuels like coal, oil, and gas. For many citizens, the experience is a wake-up call. Susanne, a resident of Vienna, told AFP that she hopes politicians will finally understand the urgency of the situation and begin to set a course in the right direction to protect the environment.
paragraphs.zh: 科學家表示，這場史上最嚴重且範圍最廣的熱浪，唯有在燃燒煤炭、石油與天然氣等化石燃料所驅動的氣候危機下才可能發生。對許多公民而言，這次經歷是一記警鐘。維也納居民蘇珊（Susanne）告訴法新社（AFP），她希望政治人物能最終理解局勢的緊迫性，並開始設定正確的方向以保護環境。

paragraphs.en: As the heat continues to affect millions, authorities across the region are urging people to stay indoors during the hottest hours of the day and to drink plenty of water. While the worst of the heat has started to ease in some western areas, the coming days remain critical for countries in the east. The combination of record temperatures and the tragic loss of life has left many across Europe asking what can be done to prepare for such events in the future.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著高溫持續影響數百萬人，該地區的當局正敦促民眾在一天中最熱的時段待在室內，並多喝水。雖然西歐部分地區的酷熱已開始緩解，但接下來的幾天對東歐國家而言依然關鍵。破紀錄的高溫與悲慘的人命損失，讓歐洲各地許多人不禁詢問，未來該如何準備以應對此類事件。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To take care of someone and make sure they are safe.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 照顧、照料，確保某人平安。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此片語非字面上的「向外看」，而是指關心他人。文中：asking citizens to look out for one another during these difficult days.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To have no more of something left.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於日常對話，但在新聞中描述資源匱乏時很關鍵。文中：mortuaries in the city have run out of space, leaving families in distress as they struggle to find places to store their loved ones.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To refuse to allow someone to enter a place or receive a service.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 拒絕（某人）進入或使用服務。
keyTerms.noteZh: 此處並非「轉身離開」，而是指拒絕提供服務。文中：noting that he has had to turn away many families because his cold rooms are completely full.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To break open or apart suddenly, especially due to internal pressure.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見於氣球破裂，這裡形容河流因水位過高而溢出。文中：Firefighters were forced to rescue people from their homes and garages after rivers rose quickly and burst their banks.

sources.name: The Guardian World
sources.title: Dangerous temperatures forecast for parts of Europe as heatwave moves east

sources.name: MedPage Today
sources.title: Europe's Record Heat Has Overwhelmed Paris Mortuaries and Left Families in Distress

previousArticles.summaryEn: A severe heatwave is moving across Europe, bringing record-breaking temperatures to central and eastern countries. Authorities have issued red alerts as cities like Budapest expect to reach 40 degrees Celsius. Meanwhile, France is dealing with the tragic aftermath of the heat, which caused over 1,000 excess deaths. Funeral directors in Paris report that mortuaries are completely full due to the high number of fatalities. Scientists warn that this extreme weather is a direct result of the ongoing climate crisis.

previousArticles.title: Europe Struggles as Record-Breaking Heatwave Moves East
previousArticles.summaryEn: Europe is experiencing a dangerous heatwave that has set new temperature records in several countries. Germany, Poland, and the Czech Republic have all recorded temperatures exceeding 40 degrees Celsius. Health officials report over 1,300 excess deaths linked to the extreme heat. Authorities are urging residents to stay hydrated and avoid strenuous activity. Climate change is being cited as a primary driver for these increasingly frequent and severe weather events.

previousArticles.title: Europe Faces Record-Breaking Heatwave as Temperatures Soar
previousArticles.summaryEn: Europe is currently experiencing an intense and dangerous heatwave that has set new temperature records in several countries. Germany, Poland, and the Czech Republic have all reported temperatures exceeding 40 degrees Celsius. Health officials are concerned about the rising number of deaths, particularly among older people. Authorities have issued warnings and taken emergency measures to help residents stay cool. Experts link this extreme weather to the ongoing effects of climate change.

quiz.question: What specific measure did the Hungarian government take to ensure the stability of the power grid during the heatwave?

options.A: They mandated that all citizens turn off their air conditioning units.
options.B: They allowed the Paks nuclear power plant to adjust its cooling water regulations.
options.C: They imported extra electricity from neighboring countries to meet demand.
options.D: They closed down non-essential industries to save energy for cooling centers.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，匈牙利政府為了確保電力供應穩定，給予 Paks 核電廠特殊許可，允許其調整冷卻水的使用規定。選項 A 文章未提及；選項 C 為無中生有；選項 D 雖然文中提到設立冷卻中心，但並未提及關閉工業以節省能源。

quiz.question: Based on the information provided, why might the current death toll estimates in France be considered inaccurate?

options.A: Many deaths are being attributed to storms rather than the heatwave.
options.B: The government is intentionally hiding data to prevent public panic.
options.C: The reliance on paper-based record-keeping likely causes delays and undercounting.
options.D: Funeral directors are refusing to report deaths due to a lack of storage space.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第五段，官員認為目前的死亡人數預估偏低，因為許多紀錄仍以紙本形式保存，而非電子化，這導致數據整合與統計速度較慢。選項 A 與文章事實不符；選項 B 屬於過度推論，文中並未暗示政府隱瞞；選項 D 雖然提到殯儀館空間不足，但這並非導致統計數字不準確的原因。

quiz.question: In the context of the article, what does the word 'catastrophic' imply when used by the mortuary owner?

options.A: A situation that is completely unmanageable and disastrous.
options.B: A temporary problem that will be solved by new equipment.
options.C: An event that is scientifically interesting but not dangerous.
options.D: A process that is moving too slowly to be effective.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 A。在第四段中，殯儀館老闆形容遺體存放空間不足的情況為「catastrophic」（災難性的），意指情況已失控且後果嚴重，無法正常運作。選項 B 與文章描述的困境矛盾；選項 C 錯誤解讀了該詞的嚴重性；選項 D 雖然提到速度問題，但未捕捉到該詞所強調的「災難性」與「嚴重程度」。

quiz.question: What is the primary message conveyed by the article regarding the recent extreme weather in Europe?

options.A: The heatwave is an isolated event that only affects the infrastructure of Eastern European countries.
options.B: Technological advancements in cooling systems have successfully mitigated the impact of climate change.
options.C: Europe is facing a multifaceted climate crisis that requires urgent action and better preparation.
options.D: The recent storms in Italy have caused more long-term damage than the heatwave itself.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章綜合討論了熱浪造成的生命損失、基礎設施損壞、極端天氣（如洪水）以及科學家對氣候變遷的警告，並呼籲採取行動，強調歐洲正面臨氣候危機帶來的多重挑戰，需要更好的準備。選項 A 忽略了西歐受到的重大影響；選項 B 與事實不符，因為災情慘重；選項 D 無法從文中得出，文章主要焦點仍是熱浪。
